text: Gamma-glycine (γ-glycine), chemical formula C2H5NO2, molecular weight 75.07, is a single-component organic molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar trigonal space group P3_1 (No. 144), with unit cell a = b = 7.037 Å, c = 5.478 Å and γ = 120°, Z = 3. The structure is built from hydrogen-bonded glycine zwitterions. The crystal is piezoelectric: piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM) on the as-grown (100) face measured an effective shear (in-plane, d15) coefficient of about 1 pm/V and an effective longitudinal (out-of-plane, d33) coefficient of about 3 pm/V. Local mechanical polishing enhanced these roughly five-fold and three-fold, to about 5 and 10 pm/V respectively, and switching-spectroscopy PFM (SS-PFM) revealed local ferroelectric switching in the polished regions with a coercive field of about 3 GV/m. Gamma-glycine is biocompatible. The crystal structure corresponds to CSD refcode GLYCIN18 (CCDC 1169370), determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 2.29% and a density of 1.592 g/cm3. The piezoelectric and local ferroelectric characterization was reported by Ivanov et al. in Physica Scripta (2023).
property_ref_title: Local piezo- and ferroelectric properties of γ-glycine single crystal observed via piezoresponse force microscopy
